pattern: Precompute a list of every index where x appears, then answer each query with a direct index lookup (1-indexed) into that list.
hints: Separate the concerns: first collect all positions where x occurs, then answer queries against that collection.
The k-th occurrence (1-indexed) is simply occurrences[k-1] if that index exists.
Build a list of indices where nums[i] == x in one pass; for each query q return occurrences[q-1] if q <= len(occurrences), else -1.

edge_inputs.description: x does not appear at all
x is the only element, repeated
mix of valid and out-of-range queries
first element is x, queries include 1 and 2
multiple x occurrences scattered in array

stress.description: large array with x appearing frequently, many queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    x = rng.randint(1, 10)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, size // 10)
    queries = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(q)]
    return [nums, queries, x]
